ans a half-lunar day. There are altogether II Karanas
distributed among the 30 Tithis in a lunar month at the rate of 2 Karanas
for each Tithi. Out of the 11 Karanas four viz. (1) Catu;p3d, (2) Naga,
(3) Kimstughna, and (4) Sakuna, arc termed Sthira or Stable, which means
that their sway is constant : They rule over the 4 half Tithis in order viz.,
(1) The latter half of the I4th lunar day in the dark half, (2) first half of
AmSvisyi, (3) second half of the same, and (4) first half of Pratipad (1st
lunar day) in the bright half. The remaining 7 are called Cara or movable.
They are (1) Bava, (2) Balava, (3) Kaulava, (4) Taitila, (5) Garaja, (ft) Vapij
and (7) Vis)! or Bhadra. These are repeated 8 times in a lunar month
beginning from the latter half of ihe first Tithi in the bright fortnight.
Remember that the places assigned to the Stable Karanas should not be
tampered with. If these principles are known for arriving at the lunar day
